Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Johan Pauwels Date: 18/12/2023 Cegeka reference: PRD.0.L.202 DV1.1 CONFIDENTIAL Service Description PRD.0.L.202 Oracle Engineered System Management Table of contents 1 Service Description 4 1.1 Service identification 4 1.2 Service summary 4 1.2.1 Infrastructure management 4 1.2.1 The Infrastructure 5 1.3 Service features 6 1.3.1 Standard Service features 6 1.3.1.1 Infrastructure knowledge 6 1.3.1.2 Technical setup 6 1.3.1.3 Technical management 6 1.3.1.4 Housekeeping 7 1.3.1.5 Monitoring 8 1.3.1.6 Security and Compliance 9 1.3.1.7 Backup and Restore 9 1.3.1.8 Incident and problem management 9 1.3.1.9 Customers responsibility 9 1.3.2 Optional Service features 10 1.3.3 Requests 10 1.3.4 Out of scope 11 1.4 Service characteristics 12 1.4.1 Obligatory 12 1.4.2 Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 12 1.4.3 Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 13 1.4.4 Conditions 13 1.4.5 Service delivery location 13 1.4.6 Service Reporting 14 1.4.7 Data classification 14 1.4.8 Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer 14 1.4.9 Miscellaneous 14 1.4.10 RACI Matrix 15 1.5 Adherence to Cross-Functional Services 17 2 Service Level Agreement 18 2.1 Service Window 18 2.2 Service Level 18 3 Service Billing 19 3.1 Standard Service Billing 19 3.2 Optional Service Billing 20 3.3 Change Request Billing 20 4 Document control 21 5 Document approval 22

Section.name: Service identification
Section.Content: Service Oracle Engineered System Management Unique ID PRD.0.L.202 Service Model Infrastructure management This service is dependant of other layers which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the full stack management.

Section.name: Infrastructure management
Section.Content: The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage an Oracle Engineered system environment (The Infrastructure) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects for such platforms. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.

Section.name: The Infrastructure
Section.Content: The Oracle Engineered Systems Appliances in scope are:

Section.name: Standard Service features
Section.Content: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the Oracle Engineered Systems infrastructure in order to perform the Service. Cegeka works together with Oracle based on the Plantinum Support contract that has been signed between Oracle and the Customer. Cegeka advises in the setup of the platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Infrastructure and in line with the best practices of Oracle (MAA). Oracle Maximum Availability Architecture (MAA) consists of a set of best practices blueprints for the integrated use of Oracle’s High Availability (HA) and Disaster Recovery (DR) technologies that ensure this level of availability. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants). Cegeka advises the Customer aligned to these principles. Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Infrastructure on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working, in line with Oracle Platinum support principles. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Reactive management Administration and reporting Patching The service includes a maximum of 2 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Infrastructure. Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan. Note that Oracle provides four patches per year, but it is advised to limit this to two updates for the engineered systems due to impact on the engineered system and effort required for application testing after upgrades. The patching is vendor (Oracle) supported, offered and aligned. The Service is responsible for starting up the patching process (ie. involving Oracle Platinum support), arrange all pre-requisites (including planning, running exachecks and providing the results to Oracle), supervise the patching process executed by Oracle and plan and communicate with the Customer. The patching follows the Oracle Platinum provided process. For the patching it is required to have an active Oracle Platinum support contract for the Engineered System in scope. Cegeka will discuss the patching process proactively with the Customer, e.g. minimum 3 months before patching. The Admin switches components in each Engineered System fall outside Platinum support contract but will be patched for minor releases within this Service (execution by Cegeka). Major releases are considered upgrades that need to be scheduled and performed separately at an additional fee. The PDU components in each Engineered System (Power Distribution Units) fall outside Platinum support contract but will be patched for minor releases within this Service (execution by Cegeka). Major releases are considered upgrades that need to be scheduled and performed separately at an additional fee. The patching only applies to the Engineered System’s firmware and software up to the operating system of the Virtual Machines running on the Engineered Systems, except for PCA where it is limited to the virtualisation layer. Application, Middleware, Database and Grid patching is out of scope of this Service (and covered in the application and database management services). The included Patching only applies to minor release. Major releases (Life Cycle Management) are considered upgrades that need to be scheduled and performed separately at an additional fee. Life Cycle Management Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Infrastructure. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. Underlaying Platform hardening Not applicable for this Service. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals).

Section.name: Optional Service features
Section.Content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Section.name: Requests
Section.Content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests (no specific Service Requests for this Service) Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service. Specifically:

Section.name: Obligatory
Section.Content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Section.name: Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to (none specific)

Section.name: Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks Oracle Engineered Systems licenses Administration of licenses involves: License compliancy Customer has to share the available licenses with Cegeka. Cegeka will assist in customer license compliance by providing knowledge and reporting on use of licenses and license requirements. Cegeka ensures to implement or activate features that are only in-line with the licenses owned by the customer.

Section.name: Service delivery location
Section.Content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Section.name: Service Reporting
Section.Content: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required. Specific reports are provided:

Section.name: Data classification
Section.Content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

Section.name: Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer
Section.Content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): Oracle support Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Activity Cegeka Customer Architecture & Design Evaluate & Selection Platform (Hardware & Software) C A,R Evaluate & Selection Platform ITSCM (IT Service Continuity Management) C A,R Lifecycle Management Platform (Hardware & Software) A,R C HA/DR/Backup requirements C A,R HA/DR/Backup solution, installation and configuration A,R C License Management Platform Licensing model C,I A,R Platform Licensing model Compliance A,R C,I Plan & Deliver Platform Network & Connectivity A,R I Co-Location Network & Connectivity A,R I Co-Location Housing A,R I Installation & Provisioning Platform (Hardware & Software) A,R C Implement Platform Backup A,R C Change Management A,R C Request Fulfilment A,R I Housekeeping (manage & assure) Trigger Platform Technology Refresh (Hardware & Software) (lifecycle management) A,R C Platform Operations (Hardware & Software) A,R C Firmware Patch Management A,R I Virtualisation/Hypervisor Patch Management A,R C OS Patch Management (excluding on PCA) A,R C Requesting Platform Capacity C A,R Delivering Platform Capacity A,R I Platform Monitoring (availability, performance, backup status) & Alerting A,R C Operate & Maintain Platform Backup A,R I Incident Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R I Problem Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R I Evaluate and assess patch and upgrade effect on infrastructure A,R C Functional testing after deploying patches and upgrades C A,R Security Platform Technology security A,R C Advise and follow up of security and auditing measures C A,R Security Patch Management A,R C RACI role definition:

Section.name: Adherence to Cross-Functional Services
Section.Content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Anti-Malware management This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the OS management layer, when applicable The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these KPI’s are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.0.L.202.Gold+ Gold+ (Multiple Engineered Systems in redundant setup for functionality) Gold % availability in Service Window KPI.0.L.202.Silver Silver (single engineered system) Silver % availability in Service Window The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as:

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these billing elements are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.0.L.202.L01 Oracle Engineered System Management Engineered System management – Exadata appliance - engineered system Engineered system (including network switches and PDU) SKU.0.L.202.L02 Engineered System management – Exadata appliance –devices Device SKU.0.L.202.L11 Engineered System management – PCA appliance - engineered system Engineered system (including network switches and PDU) SKU.0.L.202.L12 Engineered System management – PCA appliance –devices Device SKU.0.L.202.L21 Engineered System management – ZFS appliance - engineered system Engineered system (including network switches and PDU) SKU.0.L.202.L22 Engineered System management – ZFS appliance –devices Device SKU.0.L.202.L31 Engineered System management – ZDLRA appliance - engineered system Engineered system (including network switches and PDU) SKU.0.L.202.L32 Engineered System management – ZDLRA appliance –devices Device SKU.0.L.202.L33 Engineered System management – OASG applicance – physical Physical Server SKU.0.L.202.L34 Engineered System management – OASG applicance – virtual Virtual Server Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: Change Request Billing
Section.Content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Section.name: Document control
Section.Content: Document version Date Author Notes DV1.0 13/02/2024 Johan Pauwels First version aligned with structural Service Description requirements. DV1.1 25/10/2024 Johan Pauwels Comments removed, document cleanup for PDF.
